title: Ports, Protocols, Services, and Traffic Types · Protocol Recall
summary: Build and diagnose a service-access matrix from client, firewall, resolver, server, and capture evidence. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a verified protocol and service matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use N10-009 standard ports for file transfer, remote administration, mail, DNS, DHCP, web, time, monitoring, directory, database, desktop, and voice services.
Compare secure and insecure service alternatives such as SSH or Telnet, HTTPS or HTTP, LDAPS or LDAP, and SFTP or FTP.
Explain how DNS, DHCP, NTP, PTP, and NTS dependencies affect authentication, addressing, logging, and service reachability.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.
